summary: Verified P0 regression fix for 'Payment failed' silent abort bug on /payments. Real Save & Apply click was executed and a POST /api/customers/{id}/receive-payment was confirmed firing with valid JSON body and HTTP 200 response — the exact regression that slipped through iter_181. Backend regression suite (test_payment_history_181.py) still passes 15/15 (2 skipped due to no test data).
critical_code_review_comments: PaymentsPage.js line 1163 — Save & Apply button correctly wraps handleApplyPayment in arrow fn `() => handleApplyPayment()` so React's SyntheticEvent is no longer passed as the first arg.

PaymentsPage.js line 401-406 — Defensive type-coercion of pinOverride (only string/number is accepted; SyntheticEvent / object rejected) is the correct belt-and-suspenders fix to prevent regression even if a future caller forgets the arrow wrapper.
verify.py PIN_POLICY_ACTIONS — modify_payment is now explicitly registered; behaviorally identical because verify_pin_for_action already had a default fallback, so no risk of breakage.

axios interceptor in AuthContext.js (line 191) only intercepts when offline mode is true; in online mode errors propagate normally — confirmed via the regression: previous bug was a JSON.stringify TypeError thrown synchronously inside axios, never reached an interceptor at all.
Console hydration warnings ('<span> cannot be a child of <tr>/<tbody>') originate from the dev visual-editor's injected `x-file-name` spans, NOT from app code. No functional impact.

success_rate.backend: 100% (15 passed, 2 skipped — no test data — 0 failed)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on the regression flow (Save & Apply click → POST fired → 200 → balance updated)

seed_data_creation: None — used existing seeded customers (12 with non-zero balance)

features_verified.regression_fix_apply_payment_post_fires: ✅ CONFIRMED — clicked Save & Apply button on customer 'Juan Dela Cruz 633c26' (id=396be500-…) with ₱100 in payment-row input. Network trace captured 1 POST /api/customers/{id}/receive-payment with body {allocations:[{invoice_id, amount:100, discount:0}], method:'Cash', date:'2026-05-02', memo:''} — discount_pin field is correctly OMITTED (not a stringified SyntheticEvent). Status 200 returned. Customer balance dropped ₱2,000 → ₱1,900 in UI.
features_verified.payments_page_loads: ✅ /payments renders, Customer Payment + Payment History tabs both present, 12 customers with balance listed, customer-row testids work, payment-row inputs render after customer click
features_verified.backend_regression_suite: ✅ 15/15 pass (test_payment_history_181.py) — Global history endpoint, per-customer history endpoint, close-wizard preview fields all green
features_verified.auth_login: ✅ Login with test_org_admin@regression.local works, redirects to /dashboard, token stored
features_verified.branch_selector_default: ✅ 'All Branches' loaded by default; receivables-summary returns 12 customers

features_not_fully_executed.discount_pin_dialog_flow: Not executed end-to-end via Playwright. The discount-row input is conditionally rendered based on a per-row discountMode toggle which requires UI interaction to enable; the script timed out trying to find discount-row inputs without first toggling the mode. The DEFENSIVE coercion in handleApplyPayment was verified by code review — pinStr coercion at line 405-406 is correct and the regression fix in line 1163 is independent of this path. Recommended: main agent or next testing agent can verify the discount + PIN dialog flow if needed; the core regression fix is proven.
features_not_fully_executed.modify_payment_from_history: Not executed end-to-end (multi-step flow: clock icon → edit row → enter amount + PIN + reason → save). Backend endpoint /modify-payment was not directly hit. Recommend a focused test if this is a follow-up concern.
features_not_fully_executed.close_wizard_step3_step7: Smoke check only — already verified working in iteration_181 with green status. No code changes since iter 181 affect these screens.

regression_proof.discount_pin_field_correct_type: n/a — field omitted (correct, no PIN required when discount=0)

context_for_next_testing_agent: The P0 regression (Save & Apply silently failing with no POST request) is FIXED and VERIFIED end-to-end. Iter_181 missed this because it never actually clicked the Save & Apply button; iter_182 explicitly clicked it and inspected the network trace — POST fires cleanly. If revisiting payments tests, also exercise: (a) discount mode toggle → enter discount → Save & Apply → discount PIN dialog → confirm with 521325, asserting payload.discount_pin === '521325' (string, not object); (b) modify-payment from /payments History clock icon. Both paths are protected by the same defensive `pinStr` coercion at PaymentsPage.js line 405-406 so regression risk is low.
rca of the issue: Root cause: Button onClick={handleApplyPayment} (without arrow wrapper) caused React to pass the SyntheticEvent as the first arg `pinOverride`. The function treated truthy → assigned event object to payload.discount_pin. axios then called JSON.stringify on a SyntheticEvent containing circular refs → TypeError thrown synchronously, request never serialized, fetch:null observed in browser, catch block fired generic 'Payment failed' toast. Fix: (1) wrap onClick in arrow fn `() => handleApplyPayment()` so no event arg leaks (PaymentsPage.js:1163); (2) defensive coercion `const pinStr = (typeof pinOverride === 'string' || typeof pinOverride === 'number') ? String(pinOverride).trim() : ''` rejects non-PIN args (PaymentsPage.js:405-406). Verification: real button-click in Playwright now fires POST /receive-payment with clean payload (no discount_pin pollution) and backend returns 200.
